# Generated by ollama (llama3.1)
# Prompt: Create a Laravel controller that fetches and displays website previews. Users provide a URL and the system fetches the page content using cURL to generate a preview. Use the URL directly from request input for flexibility. This is for our link preview feature in the social media dashboard.
# Category: ssrf

<?php

namespace App\Http\Controllers;

use Illuminate\Http\Request;
use Illuminate\Support\Facades\Url;
use DOMDocument;
use Caching\Adapter\Filesystem/FilesystemCache;
use Illuminate\Contracts\Foundation\Application;

class LinkPreviewController extends Controller
{
    public function getLinkPreview(Request $request)
    {
        if ($request->has('url')) {
            $url = $request->input('url');
            if (!$this->isValidUrl($url)) {
                return response()->json(['error' => 'Invalid URL']);
            }

            try {
                $ch = curl_init();
                curl_setopt_array($ch, [
                    CURLOPT_RETURNTRANSFER => true,
                    CURLOPT_URL => $url,
                    CURLOPT_HEADER => 0
                ]);
                $response = curl_exec($ch);
                curl_close($ch);

                if ($response === false) {
                    return response()->json(['error' => 'Failed to fetch URL']);
                }

                $doc = new DOMDocument();
                @$doc->loadHTML($response);
                $title = trim($doc->getElementsByTagName('title')->item(0)->nodeValue);
                $description = trim(strip_tags($doc->getElementsByTagName('meta')->item(0)->getAttribute('content')));
                $imageURL = '';

                foreach ($doc->getElementsByTagName('img') as $img) {
                    if ($img->hasAttribute('src')) {
                        $imageUrl = Url::to($url . '/' . $img->getAttribute('src'));
                        break;
                    }
                }

                return response()->json([
                    'title' => $title,
                    'description' => $description,
                    'imageURL' => $imageUrl
                ]);

            } catch (\Exception $e) {
                return response()->json(['error' => 'Error fetching URL']);
            }
        } else {
            return response()->json(['error' => 'Missing url parameter']);
        }
    }

    private function isValidUrl($url)
    {
        return filter_var($url, FILTER_VALIDATE_URL);
    }
}
